data.subject: Floor Update – 3 Votes
data.bodyText:
At approximately 1:30 p.m., the House will reconvene and take the following votes:

  1. Motion on Ordering the Previous Question (H.Res. 1345) – Democrats are urged to vote no on the Previous Question. Voting no will allow House Democrats to offer an amendment to the Rule that would make in order Rep. Raskin's amendment prohibiting anyone who was convicted of or pleaded guilty to assaulting a law enforcement officer during the insurrection at the Capitol on January 6, 2021, from receiving a taxpayer-funded settlement – VOTE NO – 15 minutes
  2. H.Res. 1345 – Rule providing for consideration of S. 2 – Secure America Act; H.R. 8312 – Fraud Prevention and Accountability Act; H.R. 8464 – Stopping Fraudulent Payments Act; and H.Res. 1335 – Condemning actors seeking to defraud the United States Government, and expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that governmentwide fraud and improper payment prevention reforms will meaningfully improve the financial prosperity of the United States, and that Federal program eligibility should be verified before payment – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  3. Democratic Motion to Discharge H.Res. 1140 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5408) to accelerate workplace time-to-contract under the National Labor Relations Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition) (Rep. Norcross – Rules) – VOTE YES – 5 minutes

After the first vote series, if the Democratic Motion to Discharge is adopted, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Res. 1140 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5408) to accelerate workplace time-to-contract under the National Labor Relations Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition).

Following debate on H.Res. 1140, the House will begin one hour of debate on S. 2 – Secure America Act.

After debate on S. 2, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 7892 – No Aid for Ghost Students Act of 2026.

At approximately 4:30 p.m., the House will take a second series of votes.

Following the second vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 5408 – Faster Labor Contracts Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition).

At approximately 6:30 p.m., the House will take a vote on passage of H.R. 5408. This will be the last expected vote of the day.

Next votes predicted: at approximately 4:30 p.m., on passage of H.R. 7892, the motion to commit and passage of S. 2, and adoption of H.Res. 1140.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Schedule Update
data.bodyText:
The House has completed debate on H.R. 1329 – Smithsonian American Women’s History Museum Act.

The House has now begun one hour of debate on H.R. 6047 – Sharri Briley and Eric Edmundson Veterans Benefits Expansion Act of 2026.

Following debate on H.R. 6047, the House will recess.

At approximately 4:30 p.m., the House will reconvene and take votes. These will be the last votes of the week.

Next/Last votes predicted: at approximately 4:30 p.m., on the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 1329; adoption of H.Con.Res. 86 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran; the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 1041 – Veterans 2nd Amendment Protection Act; and passage of H.R. 6047.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Update – 6 Votes
data.bodyText:
At approximately 11:30 a.m., the House will take the following votes:

  1. Adoption of H.Con.Res. 40 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran – VOTE YES – 15 minutes
  2. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6409 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  3. Passage of H.R. 6409 – FENCES Act (Rep. Pfluger – Energy and Commerce) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes 
  4. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6398 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  5. Passage of H.R. 6398 – RED TAPE Act (Rep. Joyce (PA) – Energy and Commerce) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  6. Adoption of H.Res. 965 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 1689) to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition) – VOTE YES – 5 minutes

After the first vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 1689 – To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition).

At approximately 1:30 – 1:45 p.m., the House will take a second series of votes.

Following the second vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on the Rule providing for consideration of H.R. 8035 – To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes.

At approximately 3:15 p.m., the House will take a third series of votes on the Rule.

After the third vote series, if the Rule is adopted, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 8035 – To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes.

At approximately 5:00 p.m., the House will take a vote on passage of H.R. 8035. This will be the last expected vote of the day.

Next votes predicted: at approximately 1:30 – 1:45 p.m., on passage of H.R. 1689 – To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition) and adoption of H.Res. 1156 – Expressing support for tax policies that support working families.

data.subject: Floor Schedule Update
data.bodyText:
The House has completed debate on H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027.

The House has now begun one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 113 – Establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036.

Following debate on H.Con.Res. 113, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act.

At approximately 3:30 p.m., the House will take votes. These will be the last votes of the day.

After last votes, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 89 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran.

Any recorded vote requested on H.Con.Res. 89 will be postponed.

Next/Last votes predicted: at approximately 3:30 p.m., on pending amendments, the motion to recommit, and final passage of H.R. 8800, the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 7008, and adoption of H.Con.Res. 113.

**Members are advised that the text of S. 1383 – SAVE America Act will be added to the NDAA upon passage of the NDAA on the Floor.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Schedule Update
data.bodyText:
The House has resumed consideration of amendments to H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027. As of last night, the House has completed debate through Onder #27. Members may track consideration of amendments HERE.

After debate on H.R. 8800, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 113 – Establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036.

Following debate on H.Con.Res. 113, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act.

At approximately 3:30 p.m., the House will take votes. These will be the last votes of the day.

After last votes, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 89 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran.

Any recorded vote requested on H.Con.Res. 89 will be postponed.

Next/Last votes predicted: at approximately 3:30 p.m., on pending amendments, the motion to recommit, and final passage of H.R. 8800, the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 7008, and adoption of H.Con.Res. 113.

**Members are advised that the text of S. 1383 – SAVE America Act will be added to the NDAA upon passage of the NDAA on the Floor.

data.subject: Floor Schedule Update
data.bodyText:
Today, the House meets at 11:00 a.m. for legislative business. There will be up to fifteen “One Minutes” per side.

Following “One Minutes,” the House will complete consideration of amendments to H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027. As of last night, the House has completed debate through Onder #27. Members may track consideration of amendments HERE.

After debate on H.R. 8800, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 113 – Establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036.

Following debate on H.Con.Res. 113, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act.

At approximately 3:30 p.m., the House will take votes. These will be the last votes of the day.

Next/Last votes predicted: at approximately 3:30 p.m., on pending amendments, the motion to recommit, and final passage of H.R. 8800, adoption of H.Con.Res. 113, and the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 7008.

**Members are advised that debate on legislation related to an Iran War Powers Resolution is expected following votes today.

**Members are further advised that the text of S. 1383 – SAVE America Act will be added to the NDAA upon passage of the NDAA on the Floor.

data.subject: Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act
data.bodyText:
Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act – offered by Ms. Garcia of Texas.

If the Democratic Motion to Recommit is adopted, House Democrats will offer an amendment that would ensure the included deregulatory provisions do not apply to megabanks. Text can be found HERE.

Democrats are urged to VOTE YES on the Motion to Recommit.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Update – 18 Votes – Last Votes
data.bodyText:
The House has reconvened and is now taking the following votes on H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027:

  1. Boebert/Roy #1 – Strikes section 1213 relating to the deadline for the Afghanistan War Commission's final report, and maintains the current statutory reporting deadline – 15 minutes
  2. Boebert/Roy #2 – Strikes section 521 and replaces it with a prohibition on foreign nationals attending the United States Military Academies – 2 minutes
  3. Boebert/Roy #3 – Strikes section 524, which removes the existing statutory limit on the number of cadets who may receive alternative service obligations to pursue professional athletic careers, thereby preserving the current cap – 2 minutes
  4. Boebert/Roy #4 – Strikes section 518, relating to the pilot program on modernization of drug testing using voice-based risk assessment technology to identify servicemembers for drug testing – 2 minutes
  5. Hunt #6 – Amends the NDAA to ensure that no court may issue injunctions that impair military readiness, military fuel supply, or defense-related logistical support – 2 minutes
  6. Hunt #7 – Amends the NDAA to protect a critical component of California's defense fuel supply chain by ensuring the continued operation of the Santa Ynez Pipeline System, which transports domestically produced crude oil to refineries that supply Department of Defense installations – 2 minutes
  7. Gallagher #8 – Opens up a study to be conducted to explore mechanisms by which federal authority can preempt or otherwise mitigate state actions that effectively undermine domestic refining capacity critical to national defense. Improves transparency on how state fuel policies may impact national security, and how the Federal government may intervene – 2 minutes
  8. Carter (GA) #9 – Preempts state and local laws that impose requirements different from federal standards on certain defense-related recycling, disposal, and materials recovery activities. Specifically, it targets the mechanical processing of scrap metal and the disposition or recycling of defense articles, including defense equipment, materials, and munitions subject to demilitarization or disposal, so long as state rules are not more stringent or different than the applicable federal framework – 2 minutes
  9. Crane #14 – Prohibits the Department of Defense from using taxpayer funds to cover the costs of foreign military personnel participating in bilateral or multilateral military exercises conducted with the United States. The amendment allows the Secretary of Defense to waive the prohibition when necessary to support a U.S. national security interest, provided the Secretary submits a detailed justification and notification to the House and Senate Armed Services Committees at least 30 days in advance – 2 minutes
  10. Crane #15 – Prohibits funds for Ukraine Security Assistance except for U.S. embassy security in Ukraine – 2 minutes
  11. Crank #17 – Establishes a presumption of approval for qualified DoD servicemembers and civilian employees seeking authorization from their facility commander to carry personal firearms on military installations – 2 minutes
  12. Boebert #18 – Codifies Executive Order 14183 implements a ban on transgender service members by requiring all personnel to serve in accordance with their biological sex, citing military readiness and discipline – 2 minutes
  13. Mace #19 – Prohibits gender related medical care under TRICARE. Prevents TRICARE from covering certain gender related medical procedures and treatments – 2 minutes
  14. Mace #20 – Prohibits male participation in female sports at DoDEA schools – 2 minutes
  15. McDowell #26 – Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to establish a school choice pilot program for members of the Armed Forces – 2 minutes

    Following these votes, the House will take the following votes:
     
  16. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  17. Passage of H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act (Rep. Hill – Financial Services) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  18. Passage of H.R. 9770 – Continuing Appropriations Act, 2027 (Rep. Cole – Appropriations) – 5 minutes

These are the last votes for the day
